one_line_thesis: Nucor is a best-in-class cyclical steel compounder with a vertically integrated EAF model and a heavy but largely funded growth capex wave.

deep_dive.executive_summary: Nucor is a best-in-class cyclical steel compounder with a vertically integrated EAF model and a heavy but largely funded growth capex wave. [Fact] FY2025 was strong: $7.52 diluted earnings per share, $3.23 billion operating cash flow, and moderate leverage (24.4% funded debt to capital). [Fact] Our proof-complete component schedule values one share at about $179 on a through-cycle base versus $231 today, implying about −3.5% per year over seven years. [Inference] Downside looks partially bounded by net cash flexibility and an undrawn revolver, but steel spreads can compress quickly. We prop...

investment_committee.strongest_dissent: The valuation may capitalize an exceptional policy-and-demand environment while also crediting projects whose own supply contribution causes the margin normalization.
unresolved_items: Maintenance capex and full-cycle segment cash earnings are not independently reconciled.

decision.primary_power_zone: Capital cycle and normalized industry economics
decision.next_action: Close critical evidence gaps before freezing a decision-grade packet.

scenario_contract.rule: Cases must change cited causal operating, capital, probability, timing, or financing drivers—not only a terminal multiple.

top_value_drivers.label: Steel mills through-cycle value
top_value_drivers.scenario_assumptions: Normalize shipments, spread, utilization, maintenance capital, and tax across a full steel cycle rather than capitalizing 2025 or spot margins.

top_value_drivers.label: Downstream steel products
top_value_drivers.scenario_assumptions: Value downstream earnings separately using normalized backlog, margins, and maintenance capital.

top_value_drivers.label: New-project ramp and replacement-cost optionality
top_value_drivers.scenario_assumptions: Credit only probability-weighted after-tax cash returns above the cost of capital, net of remaining spend and ramp losses.

top_value_drivers.label: Raw materials and recycling
top_value_drivers.scenario_assumptions: Use through-cycle DRI, scrap, and brokerage economics after sustaining capital.

top_value_drivers.label: Supply-response and downcycle reserve
top_value_drivers.scenario_assumptions: Explicitly deduct for margin mean reversion, supply response, and projects earning below their cost of capital.

options.evidence: FY2025 segment and balance-sheet bridge via NUE/research/evidence_reconciliation_2026-07-15.json; proof base 25.0/sh via risk_adjusted_milestone_value@1.0.
options.scenario_assumptions: Credit only probability-weighted after-tax cash returns above the cost of capital, net of remaining spend and ramp losses.

probability_and_timing.timing_basis: Scenario range includes delay and failure outcomes.
probability_and_timing.probability_basis: Starter probability is an explicit analyst judgment pending base-rate and milestone reconciliation.
probability_and_timing.remaining_capital_basis: Remaining capital and financing are deducted in the stated option range.

options.tax_and_realization_adjustments: Low/base/high values incorporate ownership, tax, funding, cycle, and realization adjustments described in the component evidence.
options.falsifier: Primary evidence or through-cycle results support economics below the low case or reveal an omitted claim.

optionality.rule: Every option requires a beneficiary, probability, timing, remaining capital, failure case, and non-overlap control; absence of option value must be explicit rather than unvalued.

committee.strongest_dissent: The valuation may capitalize an exceptional policy-and-demand environment while also crediting projects whose own supply contribution causes the margin normalization.
unresolved_items: Maintenance capex and full-cycle segment cash earnings are not independently reconciled.
committee.next_action: Close the committee's primary-evidence blockers before requesting an owner decision.

gaps.question: Maintenance capex and full-cycle segment cash earnings are not independently reconciled.
gaps.evidence_required: Resolve the committee's missing fact with primary evidence and update the affected valuation proof row.
gaps.acceptance_test: The committee item is explicitly closed, sourced, and reflected in the valuation range or documented as immaterial.

method_fit.label: Capital cycle and normalized industry economics
applicability_tests: Industry capacity, additions, closures, and replacement costs can be measured.
Current margins can be located relative to a cycle rather than extrapolated.
Maintenance and growth capital are separated.
Leverage and liquidity survive the low case.
failure_modes: replacement cost used for assets that cannot earn their cost of capital

validation_cohort.purpose: Test depletion, commodity exposure, finite reserves, and royalty-unit reconciliation.
Test tangible capital, credit losses, funding, regulation, and excess-capital realization.
Test owner earnings, capital-light reinvestment, cyclicality, and buyback value creation.
Test normalized margins, industry supply response, replacement cost, and reinvestment timing.
Test product concentration, pipeline probabilities, patent cliffs, and catalyst timing.

method_fit.rule: Use one primary method and no more than two corroborating methods; irrelevant methods and personas remain silent.

outcomes.weighting_rule: Do not change a persona's relevance within a power zone until at least 20 attributable, dividend-aware outcomes exist there; never reweight automatically.

attribution.explanation: Component changes are classified from observable differences in quantity, comparable method, probability, timing, capital, evidence, and schedule scope.

component_valuation.decision_rule: Use the complete low/base/high component schedule. Embedded components are estimated but not added twice.

economic_value.accounting_reference: Nucor FY2025 results: 230.9m diluted shares, segment pretax income of $2.383bn steel mills, $1.229bn steel products, and $153m raw materials; $2.699bn cash/investments and $7.121bn debt/lease obligations.

economic_claim.description: One diluted share of NUE, including every identified operating claim, asset, liability, and option.
economic_claim.unit_source: Nucor FY2025 results: 230.9m diluted shares, segment pretax income of $2.383bn steel mills, $1.229bn steel products, and $153m raw materials; $2.699bn cash/investments and $7.121bn debt/lease obligations.
economic_claim.enterprise_to_equity_reconciliation: Operating and asset claims are valued once; cash, debt, financing, reserves, and other senior claims are included as separate components or within the stated equity basis.

personas.label: Mohnish Pabrai

personas.falsifier: No asset intensity / pricing power (moat eroding + non-croupier)

personas.falsifier: FCF yield below universe median OR base IRR below median
personas.label: Joel Greenblatt
personas.why_silent: Universe criteria not met: earnings_yield_high, quality_proxy

personas.falsifier: Operating lens only — no dated inefficiency / catalyst

personas.label: Howard Marks / Oaktree

personas.falsifier: Asset value lacks an independently evidenced realization path or is already capitalized in operating cash flow.
personas.label: Klarman / Baupost

timeline.label: Long-term debt was not reported in the prior period and is now 7,121,000,000.0 (USD thousands).
FY2025: $3.42B capex and $496M startup costs on Kentucky plate, West Virginia sheet, and Arizona melt shop; three-year capex ~$8.9B
FY2025 record steel-mill shipments 25.3M tons at 83% utilization; segment pretax $2.38B mills / $1.23B products
Balance sheet: $2.70B cash/investments, $7.12B debt/leases, undrawn $2.25B revolver; funded debt to capital 24.4%
West Virginia sheet mill disclosed at ~$3.65B net for ~3M t annual capacity; expected completion by year-end 2026
2026 capex guidance $2.5B as major projects near completion
Prior year steel-mill utilization 76% versus 83% in 2025; cyclical rebound in shipments and spreads
Leon Topalian continues as CEO; incentive pay tied to ROE, safety, and strategic project execution (DEF 14A cycle)
Multi-year growth program: new sheet, plate, rebar, towers, and coating capacity announced across U.S. footprint
Nucor maintains dividend through prior steel downturn; balance-sheet conservatism versus integrated peers
Section 232 tariffs reshape U.S. steel trade; Nucor benefits as domestic EAF leader while import risks persist

industry.structure: U.S. steel is concentrated among Nucor, Cleveland-Cliffs, U.S. Steel (now Cliffs-owned), and Steel Dynamics in flat and long products, with Nucor the largest EAF producer. Pricing follows global commodity spreads, scrap/DRI input costs, and import arbitrage; no single producer sets price.
industry.share_shift: EAF share of U.S. production continues to rise versus integrated blast-furnace capacity; Nucor gains on cost position and downstream integration (fabrication, rebar, joist, deck) while new domestic sheet capacity from multiple players increases supply-response risk.
industry.trend: Infrastructure and reshoring support domestic demand, but 2024–2026 brings a wave of new sheet and plate capacity, high startup costs, and volatile scrap/spread economics; AI/data-center and energy project demand partially offsets traditional construction cycles.

top_claims.claim: Leadership / governance on watch. Nucor CEO to Review Steel Results in July 28 Online Call - Stock Titan
EPS (basic) growth moved from +150.5% to +382.1% YoY for two consecutive quarters. Trailing-twelve-month growth confirms the same direction. Source: SEC XBRL quarterly filings.
Growth regime upshift: YoY growth moved to -3.0% to +382.1% YoY (baseline median -37.0% YoY) for two consecutive quarters. Source: SEC XBRL quarterly filings.

risk_claims.claim: *Primary risk: Global and domestic steel supply additions plus import pressure compress spreads while startup and project costs remain elevated. [Fact/inference] Source: `NUE/investor-documents/sec-edgar/10-K_2026_rpt20251231.htm` Item 1A.
Project execution: West Virginia or other major projects exceed budget and fail to reach peer utilization (`NUE/research/evidence_reconciliation_2026-07-21.md` falsifier).
Utilization shock: Industry utilization falls below 70% for multiple quarters while spreads shrink (10-K risk factors).
